rows.sat_rat: Maintenance costs are somewhat foreseeable from vendor schedules, but consumable burn rates in fill-finish are often underestimated.

rows.stk: Operations / Capital Budget Owner (Director of Manufacturing)
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variance between quoted and final landed acquisition cost of the capability
rows.imp_rat: Variance between quoted and landed cost erodes the budget accuracy this envelope-owner is measured against.
rows.sat_rat: Landed-cost estimation is fairly mature, though freight, tariffs, and install variances still surprise the final figure.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of losing my colleagues' trust because someone came to harm during my response
rows.imp_rat: Losing colleagues' trust after someone is harmed matters to a protector's standing, yet it is a consequence downstream of the core task.
rows.sat_rat: When responses go by the book, trust is generally maintained, so the status quo meets this moderately well.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being seen as the responder who failed to get exposed staff out and decontaminated
rows.imp_rat: Being seen as the responder who failed to extract and decon staff threatens professional standing, but reputation trails the functional stakes.
rows.sat_rat: Established protocols and team backup usually let responders perform their role competently, so this reputational failure is relatively uncommon.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the recorded sequence of response steps taken
rows.imp_rat: Clear step-sequence records aid investigation and defensibility but are peripheral to limiting harm in the moment.
rows.sat_rat: Reconstructed timelines are often ambiguous because sequencing is logged from memory after the response ends.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time gap between performing a response action and recording it during the incident
rows.imp_rat: Tightening the action-to-record time gap improves record accuracy but is a low priority while actively saving people.
rows.sat_rat: Recording usually happens after the fact because responders' hands and attention are on the response, leaving a persistent lag.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of appearing panicked rather than calm and capable when contamination strikes
rows.imp_rat: Appearing calm rather than panicked affects perceived competence but is the least consequential of the responder's outcomes relative to harm reduction.
rows.sat_rat: Drills and clear protocols help responders project composure, so status quo addresses this reasonably for trained staff.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to address technical questions raised by decision-makers about the recommendation
rows.imp_rat: Turnaround time answering decision-maker questions affects approval velocity but is a lower-stakes efficiency outcome.
rows.sat_rat: Well-documented submittals shorten Q&A cycles, giving middling responsiveness today.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of decision-makers or regulators questioning the authority of the containment recommendations
rows.imp_rat: Repeated questioning of recommendation authority erodes influence but is a lower-order status concern versus outright failure.
rows.sat_rat: Solid technical justification usually secures authority, though soft data invites occasional pushback.

rows.stk: Operations / Capital Budget Owner (Director of Manufacturing)
rows.stmt: Reduce the time to release allocated capital once the spill-response funding decision is approved
rows.imp_rat: Speed of releasing approved capital matters to execution but is largely an internal process this director can influence.
rows.sat_rat: Once approved, capital-release workflows are reasonably efficient at most managed sites.

rows.stk: Operations / Capital Budget Owner (Director of Manufacturing)
rows.stmt: Reduce the number of revision cycles required to make the spill-response business case approvable
rows.imp_rat: Excess business-case revision cycles cost this director time but are a process nuisance more than a core stakes issue.
rows.sat_rat: Standard capex templates reduce churn somewhat, but weak risk data still forces multiple approval loops.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of containment steps omitted from the spill response record after the event
rows.imp_rat: Complete step documentation matters for GMP compliance and investigation, but for this operator it is secondary to physically stopping the spread of the spill.
rows.sat_rat: Reconstructing steps from memory after a chaotic response routinely leaves gaps, and status-quo paper/after-action logs do little to prevent omissions.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time between performing a containment action and recording it in the spill response log during an active spill
rows.imp_rat: Real-time logging during an active spill competes directly with the operator's hands-on containment work, so it is a low priority in the moment of the job.
rows.sat_rat: Gloved, gowned operators cannot practically write while containing, so documentation is almost always deferred, meaning the status quo largely fails this outcome.
